title: Tourism Promotion Grant
description: The tourism board wants to fund a citywide marketing push. They need formal branding rights and a partnership agreement.

title: Culinary Tourism Grant
description: A food-focused marketing push to attract culinary tourists. The restaurant association demands prominent branding and event space.

title: Heritage Tourism Grant
description: Funding to promote historical landmarks across the city. Requires strict preservation guarantees in the marketing materials.

title: International Sports Event Bid
description: The city is awarded hosting rights for a major international event. Short-term budget injection — logistics costs may follow.

title: Esports Major Tournament
description: The city secures a massive international gaming tournament. Immediate sponsor cash flows in, but internet and grid infrastructure will be tested.

title: Regional Athletics Championship
description: Your city is chosen for a highly publicized regional track and field championship. The initial payout is generous to prepare the venues.

title: Local Business Summit Grant
description: The city's chamber of commerce wants to host an international business summit. They need facilities funding and official endorsement.

title: Innovation Expo Grant
description: A coalition of local startups wants to host a citywide technology expo. They require municipal backing and a prominent central venue.

title: Trade Association Conference
description: A powerful manufacturing guild wants to base its annual conference here. City endorsement and security cordons are mandatory.

title: Film Production Subsidy
description: A film studio wants to use the city as a backdrop. Tax incentives and location fees generate quick revenue — but tabloid crews come with the deal.

title: Independent Film Festival
description: A prominent indie festival wants to relocate to your city. It brings immediate cash and tourism, but the avant-garde crowds might cause friction.

title: Television Pilot Incentive
description: A major TV network wants to shoot a high-budget pilot downtown. The location fees are great, but traffic disruptions will agitate the press.

title: Cultural Exchange Program
description: UNESCO is funding a cultural exchange initiative. The PR value is significant and the public tends to respond well to international recognition.

title: International Art Biennale
description: Funding to host a prestigious art biennale. The civic pride boost is wonderful, and the initial grant makes setup highly appealing.

title: Global Music Showcase
description: A global foundation offers to sponsor a massive traditional music showcase in your city. It elevates the city's international profile safely.

title: Green Energy Infrastructure Grant
description: A 3-year renewable infrastructure grant paid in installments. The public views it favorably — your planning team will appreciate the investment.

title: Solar Grid Modernization Grant
description: A long-term grant aimed at outfitting municipal buildings with solar arrays. Multi-year installments with strong support from city planners.

title: Urban Canopy Initiative
description: A 3-year phased funding program to plant thousands of trees and build eco-corridors. Extremely popular with residents and planning officials.

title: University Research Partnership
description: A research council offers funding to establish a city-university technology lab. Your urban planning team needs to be on board for this to work.

title: Medical Research Partnership
description: Funding to construct a cutting-edge biomedical research annex. Requires direct integration with municipal health and zoning plans.

title: Engineering Innovation Lab
description: A board wants to fund a massive civil engineering testing lab. Your urban planners will have to sign off on the land usage requirements.

title: City Branding Campaign
description: An international city network will fund a major visibility push. Your city gets international exposure in exchange for co-branding rights.

title: Digital Nomad Campaign
description: An alliance wants to market your city as the ultimate remote work destination. They'll fund the campaign in exchange for city-sponsored incentives.

title: Smart City Showcase
description: A prominent forum wants to use your city as a poster child for smart technology. Generates great visibility and cash, but requires deep data sharing.

title: Tech Hub Partnership
description: A multinational consortium offers infrastructure funding in exchange for zoning rights near the city center. Residents are already skeptical.

title: Data Center Zoning Deal
description: A massive tech firm wants to build a hyper-scale data center. It brings huge capital, but it will consume massive power and displace neighborhoods.

title: Autonomous Vehicle Testing Hub
description: A tech giant wants to turn city streets into a testing ground for driverless cars. Highly lucrative, but safety concerns enrage local planners.

title: World Bank Development Loan
description: A large development loan earmarked for public infrastructure. Repayment terms are favorable — but fiscal conditionalities apply.

title: Continental Development Loan
description: A substantial loan meant strictly for upgrading civic utilities. It shores up the treasury, but the public resents the accompanying austerity terms.

title: Global Infrastructure Fund
description: A major capital injection intended to modernize transit systems. It pleases the finance department, but comes with rigid privatization demands.

title: Foreign Direct Investment Deal
description: A private equity group is willing to invest heavily in the city's growth sector. Opposition groups are already calling it a sellout.

title: Venture Capital Injection
description: A VC firm wants to aggressively fund local commercial districts. It provides massive liquidity but risks gentrifying historic neighborhoods rapidly.

title: Real Estate Consortium Deal
description: A coalition of foreign developers offers to buy up struggling municipal properties. The financial boon is undeniable, but it looks terrible to voters.

title: Corporate Sponsorship Package
description: A major corporation wants to sponsor public infrastructure in exchange for naming rights. Your finance team is enthusiastic — whether that's reassuring or not.

title: Stadium Naming Rights Deal
description: A massive holding company offers to inject cash into the city budget if they can plaster their logo on the central municipal stadium.

title: Transit System Sponsorship
description: An energy firm wants to sponsor the city's bus network, renaming lines after their products. It's an easy win financially, despite the tacky optics.

title: Municipal Infrastructure Bond
description: A 2-year government-backed bond paid in installments. Lower short-term gains but more stable than a lump sum deal.

title: Green Transit Bond
description: A 2-year state-backed bond focused entirely on mass transit. It restricts your budget flexibility slightly but pays out well over time.

title: Municipal Housing Bond
description: A phased bond distribution meant to alleviate housing crises. It brings a lot of money into the system but slightly annoys the tax base.

title: Port Authority Long-Term Lease
description: A shipping consortium wants a long-term port lease. The deal is lucrative — but labor unions are already unhappy about the terms.

title: Rail Freight Terminal Lease
description: A massive logistics firm wants to lease the central rail freight hub. The influx of cash is great, but transit workers are threatening strikes.

title: Airport Cargo Hub Lease
description: An international freight carrier offers a premium to exclusively lease parts of the municipal airport. Commercial airlines and locals will suffer the disruption.

title: Media Conglomerate Licensing Deal
description: A media group wants exclusive city broadcast rights. The money is good — but losing editorial independence over city coverage is a real risk.

title: Telecom Infrastructure Monopoly
description: A telecom giant will pay a premium to monopolize city data cables. It boosts the budget immediately but creates huge vulnerability and public outrage.

title: Citywide Advertising Exclusivity
description: An agency wants exclusive rights to all public billboards and transit ads. The visual clutter will be intense, and the press will hunt for corruption angles.

title: Technology Incubator Initiative
description: A private fund wants to establish a tech incubator. They'll only deal with a city that has a proven governance track record.

title: Biotech Research Park
description: A venture fund offers to finance a biomedical research park. They require evidence of stable local leadership before releasing the capital.

title: Green Tech Accelerator
description: An investment firm wants to build a clean energy accelerator. The terms require a proven track record of resolving civic issues first.

title: NGO Infrastructure Grant
description: A coalition of NGOs is funding basic infrastructure repairs. They want oversight access and mandatory public reporting in return.

title: Clean Water Initiative Grant
description: An international NGO offers funding specifically to repair the water grid. Accepting requires transparent audits that could expose other flaws.

title: Medical Clinic Rebuilding Fund
description: A grant to rebuild critical medical infrastructure. They demand extensive oversight to ensure the funds aren't siphoned off.

title: Diaspora Investment Fund
description: A diaspora investment program funded by citizens abroad. Three-year commitment with steady installments and strong community goodwill.

title: Expat Rebuilding Fund
description: Wealthy expatriates have pooled resources to help rebuild the city over three years. It comes with deep civic pride and slow, stable payouts.

title: Overseas Heritage Bond
description: A phased bond funded entirely by citizens living abroad. It stabilizes the budget over several cycles without typical corporate strings.

title: Military Logistics Contract
description: The defense command wants to use city infrastructure for staging. Requires active liaison coordination — and comes with political strings.

title: Naval Resupply Contract
description: The Navy wants priority access to city docks for resupply. High payout, but civilians will resent the militarization of local ports.

title: Aerospace Defense Testing Pact
description: The Air Force wants to test drone logistics over municipal airspace. Highly lucrative, deeply unpopular, and requires your military liaison to manage.

title: Emergency Aid Coalition
description: International relief funding that only reaches cities in genuine documented need — late in their governance cycle. Refusing signals you don't need it.

title: Crisis Recovery Fund
description: Massive international relief targeted at cities in dire condition. Rejecting this looks foolish to a desperate population.

title: Food Security Relief Injection
description: Critical emergency funding for supply chains near collapse. The money is fast, but turning it down carries a heavy political penalty.

title: Sovereign Wealth Fund Injection
description: A sovereign wealth fund is interested in the city's long-term assets. The investment is substantial — and so are the governance conditions attached.

title: Royal Investment Portfolio
description: A foreign monarchy offers to inject massive wealth into the city's struggling economy. The oversight demands are highly intrusive and unpopular.

title: National Resource Fund Deal
description: A state-owned resource fund wants to park extreme amounts of capital in your city's infrastructure. Citizens despise the opaque terms of the agreement.

title: IMF Stabilization Package
description: Emergency fiscal injection. There are strings attached — austerity expectations and oversight measures are part of the deal.

title: Central Bank Liquidity Facility
description: A harsh but necessary liquidity injection from the central bank. It saves the budget but forces wildly unpopular spending cuts.

title: International Currency Swap
description: An emergency mechanism to keep the city solvent. It dumps money into your treasury, but the public perceives it as a loss of economic sovereignty.

title: UN Humanitarian Aid Grant
description: Emergency humanitarian funds from the UN. The public sees this as international support — but it comes with strict reporting requirements.

title: Global Refugee Assistance Fund
description: A massive grant meant to stabilize populations in crisis. Refusing it creates a terrible public image.

title: Child Welfare Emergency Grant
description: Substantial funding aimed at protecting vulnerable youth during extreme crisis. The money is vital, and turning it down is political suicide.

title: Emergency Disaster Relief Fund
description: International relief funding following a crisis designation. The paperwork is fast-tracked — funds arrive immediately.

title: Seismic Reconstruction Fund
description: Rapidly deployed capital intended to fix catastrophic damage. Very popular with citizens and highly accessible.

title: Severe Weather Mitigation Grant
description: Emergency cash to rebuild after severe environmental destruction. The payout is swift and generates immediate goodwill.

title: International Observer Program
description: An international body offers funding in exchange for deploying independent governance observers. Declining signals the city has something to hide.

title: Election Transparency Mission
description: A group offers operational funds if they can embed monitors in city hall. Rejecting this breeds immediate suspicion.

title: Human Rights Audit Program
description: Funding granted in exchange for full access to municipal records by a rights organization. Saying no guarantees terrible press.

title: Foreign Defense Support Package
description: A security bloc offers logistical and financial support. There are geopolitical implications — the public will not unanimously approve.

title: Border Security Aid Package
description: A foreign military alliance will pay to reinforce the city's perimeter. It enrages the populace but thrills your military advisors.

title: Counter-Terrorism Operations Grant
description: An influx of capital to support harsh anti-insurgency measures in the city. The military loves it; the citizens are terrified.

title: Off-Books Financing Arrangement
description: An anonymous financial intermediary has a large sum available — no questions asked. The risk of exposure is substantial.

title: Offshore Syndicate Loan
description: A massive loan from shell companies with untraceable origins. It solves your money problems overnight, but an audit will ruin you.

title: Dark Pool Liquidity Injection
description: An illegal transfer of funds into the city treasury through a web of ghost accounts. Astounding wealth, astronomical scandal risk.

title: Territorial Access Negotiation
description: A faction leader is willing to pay for safe access to city infrastructure. Refusing may worsen tensions in already contested areas.

title: Mercenary Transit Pact
description: A rogue commander offers heavy bribes to move troops via city rail lines. It's scandalous, but rejecting them will provoke an immediate blockade.

title: Sanctioned Smuggling Route
description: Organized crime offers a cut of profits to ignore a key supply corridor. Turning them down risks explosive violence in the streets.

title: Defense Procurement Contract
description: A defense contractor wants to use the city as a logistics hub. The money is significant — but political and reputational exposure is severe.

title: Weapons Manufacturing License
description: An arms dealer wants zoning approval for a massive munitions factory in the city limits. It brings huge revenue but creates a massive target.

title: Black Market Munitions Hub
description: A notorious broker will pay top dollar to operate a gray-market supply depot out of the city. Highly illegal, incredibly lucrative.

title: Proxy Administration Agreement
description: A foreign government is willing to effectively back your administration financially. The terms are opaque. Exposure could end your term. This offer won't come again.

title: Puppet Regime Subsidy
description: An enemy nation offers virtually unlimited funding if you quietly align city policies with their geopolitical goals. Treason has never paid so well.

title: Shadow State Financing
description: A cabal of ultra-wealthy elites offers to bankroll the entire city budget. In exchange, they become the true rulers. Unprecedented wealth, apocalyptic risk.
